name: Cuminate sensor (CymR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects cuminate (p-isopropylbenzoate), a terpenoid-degradation marker. The native P. putida CymR sensor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in P. putida KT2440.

input.detects: Cuminate/cumate in degradation streams and as a process marker

sensing.sensorMechanism: CymR de-represses the Pcym/Pcmt promoter on binding cumate.
sensing.signalFlow: Cumate -> CymR releases Pcym -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with cuminate.

parts.name: CymR regulator
parts.source: Native P. putida cym/cmt operon (also a Marionette sensor)
parts.notes: Cumate-responsive repressor; endogenous to P. putida.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

performance.notes: CymR is native to P. putida and a characterised Marionette sensor; integrated CRISPRa device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 certified HV1 safety host; add containment for field use.
safety.notes: Native sensor in the host — minimal porting needed.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak reporter promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: Pcym -> sgRNA (native CymR). TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. SEVA vectors.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native P. putida CymR cumate sensor with CRISPRa
partsSources: Eaton / P. putida cym-cmt cumate regulation; Meyer et al. 2019 (Marionette CymR)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
